critique: The delivered result is an exact objective-specific implementability-gap certificate, not the full scalar exactness frontier advertised by the title-level framing. The note proves the reduction to a two-block elliptope slice, characterizes the plus-minus-one image as the parity-truncated slice, and computes Delta_m^pm by a finite active-set/truncation formula; that is enough for a field-level sharp certificate. What is not proved is a closed r_star phase boundary or a broad high-kappa iid exactness region: the iid theorem is only a knife-edge affine-balanced locus, with off-locus convergence only as kappa tends to infinity. The positive-gap theorem is also restricted to odd m and a low-scale window, so it should not be read as a complete phase diagram. These limitations keep it below flagship but do not reduce the proved kernel below field.
